intended_use: The Huxley Home Sleep Apnea Test (SANSA) is a wearable device intended for use in the recording, analysis, and storage of biophysical parameters to aid in the evaluation of sleep-related breathing and
intended_use_full: The Huxley Home Sleep Apnea Test (SANSA) is a wearable device intended for use in the recording, analysis, and storage of biophysical parameters to aid in the evaluation of sleep-related breathing and cardiac disorders of adults suspected of sleep apnea. The device is intended for the clinical and home use setting under the direction of a Healthcare Professional (HCP). The system is prescription use only. The SANSA device records and stores ECG recording for up to 10 hours of wear time which can be displayed in the software portal for manual annotation and analysis. The SANSA does not provide automated analysis of the ECG and is not intended to be used with a 3rd party automated algorithm and is not intended for pacemaker analysis.
target_condition: Sleep apnea and sleep-related breathing disorders
ground_truth: Reference standard Holter monitor for ECG clinical performance validation
other_metrics: Heart Rate accuracy: ±3 bpm (range 30-250 bpm); SpO2 accuracy: ±3% (range 70-100%); ECG Frequency Response: 0.67 Hz to 40 Hz; ECG Input Impedance: >10 MΩ; ECG Gain Accuracy: Maximum amplitude error ±10%; ECG Gain Stability: <3% over 24-hour period; ECG Timing Accuracy: <30 seconds; Aid to Diagnosis of Moderate to Severe OSA (AHI≥15): Sensitivity 88.2%, Specificity 87.3%
